meta.title: Depression Symptoms & Wellbeing Check-In
meta.description: A private 500-statement, non-diagnostic reflection across 20 depression-related areas.

levels.label: Lower Reported Symptom Load
levels.desc: You reported fewer difficulties across this non-diagnostic check-in. Continue noticing changes, especially if functioning or safety becomes affected.

levels.label: Some Reported Difficulties
levels.desc: You reported some depressive experiences. Supportive routines and a conversation with someone you trust may help; seek professional advice if symptoms persist.

levels.label: Several Areas of Concern
levels.desc: Several areas appear difficult. Consider discussing these experiences with a qualified health professional, especially if they have lasted two weeks or affect daily life.

levels.label: High Reported Symptom Load
levels.desc: Your responses suggest substantial current difficulty. Please consider contacting a qualified health professional soon and involve a trusted support person.

levels.label: Significant Reported Distress
levels.desc: You reported significant distress or impairment. Please seek professional support promptly. If you may be unsafe, use emergency or crisis support now.

questions.text: During the past two weeks, I experienced periods when my mood felt reasonably steady.
On most mornings, I felt sad, empty, or emotionally low.
While handling ordinary responsibilities, I could notice sadness without it filling the entire day.
After a disappointing event, A heavy low mood stayed with me for much of the day.
